law.text:
Chapter 4. Succession entitlement of the spouse and the legally cohabiting partner

Section 1. The surviving spouse

Art. 4.17. Inheritance rights of the surviving spouse

par. 1. When the deceased leaves descendants, adopted children or descendants of these, the surviving spouse acquires the usufruct of the entire estate.

par. 2. When the deceased leaves relatives in the ascending line or close collateral relatives, the surviving spouse acquires:
(1) the full ownership of the share of the first to die in the common property and in the property held exclusively in undivided co-ownership between the spouses, and
(2) the usufruct of the remaining goods of their own property.
(3) the usufruct of the goods subject to the right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25, unless otherwise provided in the deed of gift or in the will.

par. 3. When the deceased leaves other heirs or no heirs, the surviving spouse acquires the full ownership of the entire estate.

Art. 4.18. Usufruct over donated goods

Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is called to the estate receives the usufruct of the goods that the donor donated and of which the donor reserved the usufruct, on the condition that the spouse already held that capacity at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is called to the estate receives the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate opened and of the household effects present therein, on the condition that the donor had reserved the usufruct of those goods upon the donation of those goods, that the spouse was legally cohabiting with the donor at the time of the donation, and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

The spouse may renounce that usufruct. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to that renunciation when it takes place during the lifetime of the donor.

Art. 4.20. Right to lease

The surviving spouse acquires exclusively, to the exclusion of all other heirs, the right to the lease of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the deceased opened.

Art. 4.22. Exclusion or forfeiture

par. 1. The surviving spouse may be wholly or partially excluded from their inheritance rights or declared to have forfeited them if they have been wholly or partially deprived of parental authority over the children born of their marriage with the deceased.

par. 2. The legal action is brought by the descendants within one year calculated either from the opening of the estate or from the deprivation of parental authority.

The judgment takes effect from the date on which the action was brought.

par. 3. If the usufruct has already been converted into the full ownership of a good or into a sum of money, or if the bare ownership of the goods referred to in article 4.61, paragraph 7, has already been transferred, the exclusion or the declaration of forfeiture gives rise to a right to compensation.

That compensation is determined by the court and corresponds to the value of the usufruct, taking into account the presumed life expectancy of the usufructuary at the time the legal action was brought.

If the usufruct has been converted into a life annuity, the judgment has retroactive effect to the same point in time.

Section 2. The surviving legally cohabiting partner

Art. 4.23. Inheritance rights and right to lease of the surviving legally cohabiting partner

par. 1. The surviving legally cohabiting partner acquires, regardless of which heirs they are called to the estate with, the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the deceased opened, and of the household effects present therein.

The surviving legally cohabiting partner also exercises this right if the goods referred to in the first paragraph are the subject of a right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25.

par. 2. The surviving legally cohabiting partner who is called to the estate receives upon the death of the donor the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate opened and of the household effects present therein, if the donor had reserved the usufruct of these goods upon the donation of these goods, and on the condition that the legally cohabiting partner already held that capacity at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

The legally cohabiting partner may renounce the usufruct referred to in the first paragraph. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to that renunciation when it takes place during the lifetime of the donor.

par. 3. The surviving legally cohabiting partner acquires exclusively, to the exclusion of all other heirs, the right to the lease of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the predeceased legally cohabiting partner opened, and acquires the usufruct of the household effects present therein.

par. 4. The foregoing provisions do not apply when the surviving legally cohabiting partner is a descendant of the predeceased legally cohabiting partner.

par. 5. Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary, anyone who acquires the bare ownership may demand that an inventory of the household effects and a statement of condition of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence be drawn up.

par. 6. The rules concerning the usufruct of the surviving spouse that are included in articles 4.22 and 4.60 to 4.64 apply mutatis mutandis to the usufruct of the surviving legally cohabiting partner.

kb_used.fo:
vocabulary V {
  type Person
  type Asset
  is_surviving_spouse_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  is_deceased: Person -> Bool
  is_descendant_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  is_adopted_child_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  is_descendant_of_adopted_child_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue: Person -> Bool
  is_part_of_estate_of: Asset * Person -> Bool
  acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate: Person * Person -> Bool
}

theory T:V {
  ! deceased in Person, issue in Person: (((is_descendant_of(issue,deceased)) | (is_adopted_child_of(issue,deceased)) | (is_descendant_of_adopted_child_of(issue,deceased)))) => ((deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue(deceased))).
  ! spouse in Person, deceased in Person: (((is_surviving_spouse_of(spouse,deceased)) & (is_deceased(deceased)) & (deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue(deceased)))) => ((acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate(spouse,deceased))).
}

case.text: Peter and Sara were married. Sara dies. Peter is the surviving spouse. Sara leaves behind one minor child. There is no will.

questions.text: Does Peter have the right, pursuant to Article 4.17, paragraph 1, to the usufruct of the entire estate?

expected.reason: Art. 4.17 par. 1: when the deceased leaves descendants, the surviving spouse obtains usufruct of the whole estate.

is_surviving_spouse_of.name: is_surviving_spouse_of
is_surviving_spouse_of.description: The first person is the surviving spouse of the second person (the deceased).

is_descendant_of.name: is_descendant_of
is_descendant_of.description: The first person is a descendant of the second person (the deceased).

is_adopted_child_of.name: is_adopted_child_of
is_adopted_child_of.description: The first person is an adopted child of the second person (the deceased).

is_descendant_of_adopted_child_of.name: is_descendant_of_adopted_child_of
is_descendant_of_adopted_child_of.description: The first person is a descendant of an adopted child of the second person (the deceased).

deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue.name: deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue
deceased_leaves_qualifying_issue.description: The deceased leaves at least one descendant, adopted child, or descendant of an adopted child.

is_part_of_estate_of.name: is_part_of_estate_of
is_part_of_estate_of.description: The asset forms part of the estate of the deceased person.

acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate.name: acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate
acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate.description: The first person (surviving spouse) acquires the usufruct of the entire estate of the second person (the deceased).

routing.question: Does Peter have the right, pursuant to Article 4.17, paragraph 1, to the usufruct of the entire estate?

deduction.label: entailed
deduction.constraint: ? x0 in Person, x1 in Person: __sel0(x0) & __sel1(x1) & acquires_usufruct_of_entire_estate(x0,x1)

symbolic_intent_routing.question: Does Peter have the right, pursuant to Article 4.17, paragraph 1, to the usufruct of the entire estate?

pipeline.natural_language: Yes. Peter, Sara: The first person (surviving spouse) acquires the usufruct of the entire estate of the second person (the deceased).
